title: leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. leucolepis
intro: Leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. leucolepis, also known as the Russian daisy or Arctic daisy, is a plant species that belongs to the aster family and is native to the Arctic regions of Siberia and Russia. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its use as a diuretic and antiseptic, and is traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections and wounds. The health benefits of Leucanthemum ircutianum subsp. leucolepis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
